rulings.Case Title: CENTRAL GARDENS, INC. VS. ROBIN DEWAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday May 13, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT ROBIN DEWAR, DENNIS O'LEARY'S Motion To Reopen Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Larkin Yeager and Michael Lieberman shall be deposed on or before June 1, 2015. Expert witness discovery shall be completed before July 1, 2015 subject to a stipulation to extend the time due to Ms. Henle's travel schedule. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR TUNG VS. AMI QI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday May 13, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF VICTOR TUNG'S Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Defendant Qi has failed to meet her burden, as the propounding party, to justify 168 interrogatories. Sanctions awarded against Ms. Qi in the amount of $3,892. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WENJIE HUA VS. DAVID MING YOUNG MD et al (Defts: Regents' Defts Challenge Judge Goldsmith)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Defendants The Regents Of The University Of California, Robert Foster, M.D., And David Ming Young, M.D.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 13, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANTS DAVID YOUNG, ROBERT FOSTER, AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Per Order of the Presiding Judge, this motion is continued to May 21, 2015 at 9:00 am in Dept. 318. The Court is not in receipt of a courtesy copy of the opposition papers. Plaintiff shall deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Dept. 302 (even though the motion will be heard in Dept. 318, the papers should still be delivered to Dept. 302), with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 13, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROSALIE ZUNIGA VS. URIAH L CHU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Defendants Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday May 13, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT URIAH CHU, WILLIAM CHU Notice Of Motion And Defendants Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted as to questions 1, 2, 3 and 11. The witness was allowed to answer questions 7, 12, 13, 14, and 15 and it appears that she mostly did answer those questions. Counsel's instruction to the witness not to answer the remaining questions at issue here on the grounds of privacy was appropriate in that her privacy rights outweighed the right to discovery on those questions. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LINDSEY E. SCULLY VS. MENLO CAPITAL GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Lindsey E. Scullys Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Defendant Realty Income Corporation, Llc And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday May 13, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF LINDSEY SCULLY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Defendant Realty Income Corporation, Llc And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Having been served more than 45 days after the initial responses, the motion is untimely. Plaintiff has not filed a motion under CCP section 473 and therefore such motion for relief is not before this court. The motion to compel is denied without prejudice subject to the outcome of a CCP section 473 motion, if any. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Lindsey E. Scullys Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Defendant Sunil Suri And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday May 13, 2015, Line 5, Plaintiff Lindsey E. Scully's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Defendant Sunil Suri And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Having been served more than 45 days after the initial responses, the motion is untimely. Plaintiff has not filed a motion under CCP section 473 and therefore such motion for relief is not before this court. The motion to compel is denied without prejudice subject to the outcome of a CCP section 473 motion, if any. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LOREN M. LOPIN VS. JANE CHAVEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Plaintiff'S Request For Right To Attach Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 13, 2015, Line 12, Hearing On Plaintiff'S Request For Right To Attach Order. The motion for right to attach order and issuance of writ is granted. The requirements for obtaining a writ of attachment are: (1) the claim upon which the attachment is based is one upon which an attachment may issue; (2) plaintiff has established the probable validity of the claim; (3) the attachment is not sought for a purpose other than recovery of the claim upon which the attachment is based; (4) the amount to be secured by the attachment is greater than zero. (CCP § 484.090(a); See California Retail Portfolio Fund GmbH & Co. KG v. Hopkins Real Estate Group (2011) 193 CA4th 849, 856; Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 9(II)-D.) In the case at bar, the claim is one for money, is over $500, is unsecured, and is a commercial claim. It is more likely than not that Plaintiff will obtain a judgment against Defendant because the parties have entered into a valid legal agreement and Plaintiff is seeking enforcement of the agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
